titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to James Jackson Clark, 17 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926) to James Jackson Clark (1846-1926), a Colonel from Derry. This letter is a reply to a letter sent by Clark requesting a pass to travel to England. In this letter O'Farrell states that the Lord Lieutenant, Ivor Guest (1873-1939) has informed him that there are no passes being permitted, and that Clark should apply for a certificate from his Local District Inspector to satisfy the Military Authorities.

msIdentifier.collection: National Archive of Ireland-Chief Secretary's Office-Registered Papers, NAI/CSO/RP/1916/5611/8242/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
17th May, 1916. No. 8242. Sir Withe reference to your letter of the 15th
instant, I am directed by the Lord Lieutenant to
state that formal permits are not being issued by
the Military Authorities to enable persons to leave
Ireland. It will, however, be necessary for you to
obtain from the local District Inspector Royal Irish
Constabulary a certificate to satisfy the Military
Authorities that there are no grounds for objecting
to your departure. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant,
E O'Farrell
Colonel Jackson Clark, H.M.L., Largantogher, Maghera, Co. Derry.
